prompt: Create a vertical cinematic wuxia fantasy portrait in a dim ancient Chinese interior: a lone mysterious swordswoman stands full-body slightly left of center, wearing a flowing translucent white hanfu with many soft gauze layers and oversized sleeves, the fabric billowing gently as if caught by a breeze. She has very long loose black hair falling over her shoulders and down her back, and her face is deliberately obscured by a plain muted gray square censor block. In her lowered right hand she holds one long dark straight sword angled diagonally downward toward the lower right, with an ornate bronze-gold hilt. The room is an old carved wooden Chinese chamber with lattice windows, embroidered sheer curtains, antique furniture, carved screens, dark beams, and hanging calligraphy scrolls. Include exactly six visible warm candle flames: one large blurred candle in the lower left foreground, two candles on a left-side stand, one small candle near the back left, one candle on a right-side table, and one blurred candle glow in the lower right foreground. Shoot through soft out-of-focus foreground curtains or wooden screens on both sides, creating a voyeuristic framed composition, shallow depth of field, dust motes in the air, teal-green shadows, warm amber candle highlights, misty atmosphere, realistic fabric texture, elegant melancholic mood, 35mm film still aesthetic, slight Dutch angle, high detail, soft cinematic lighting, no text, no watermark.
